original_hypothesis.title: Enumerable role-member indexing assumptions can break owner/admin rotation if role member sets contain multiple members
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setAdministrator() and setOwner() revoke the role from getRoleMember(role, 0), assuming a single member and stable ordering. If multiple members exist (e.g., due to direct grantRole usage), these functions may revoke the wrong member and leave an unintended privileged account active.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a correctness defect that can lead to persistent hidden privileged accounts and upgrade authority ambiguity. It is practical in managed deployments where operators might use AccessControl's grantRole directly (allowed for DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE) and later use setOwner/setAdministrator expecting to rotate a single account.
deployment_assumptions: Role sets (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE or ADMIN) can contain >1 member due to operational actions (direct grantRole) or migrations
Operators rely on setOwner/setAdministrator for rotation
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment script shown setting ADMIN/MASTER_MINTER/CONTROLLER; deployToken.js calls setOwner once post-deploy but does not prevent future direct grantRole usage.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setAdministrator revokes ADMIN from getRoleMember(ADMIN, 0) then grants newAdmin; similar pattern exists for OWNER in Token.sol setOwner

preconditions: ADMIN role member count > 1 OR DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE member count > 1
Operator calls setAdministrator or setOwner expecting to rotate a single account

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: ERC20AdminUpgradeable.sol lines 27-38 (and Token.sol setOwner pattern, though not anchored here)
initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: role rotation uses index 0 of enumerable set
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: wrong account revoked; hidden admins/owners persist
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no check that getRoleMemberCount(role)==1 before rotation; no explicit 'oldAdmin' parameter

analysis_questions: Can ADMIN/OWNER role sets ever contain multiple members in intended ops?
Does any operational tooling grant roles directly via grantRole?
Should rotation functions explicitly revoke msg.sender or a passed-in oldAdmin?
validation_requirements: Unit test: grantRole(ADMIN, A) and grantRole(ADMIN, B); call setAdministrator(C) and observe which account remains
Check on-chain role member counts on deployed instances
Review governance SOPs to ensure no multi-member role states occur

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A privileged operator mistakenly grants ADMIN to a second address for maintenance. Later they call setAdministrator expecting to replace the old admin, but only index-0 member is revoked and the other admin remains with full powers (pause/blacklist/fees/forwarder).

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Moderate to high: lingering admin can alter fees/forwarder or freeze/forceTransfer (depending on remaining privileges)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users if a hidden admin persists
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Medium: must enumerate and revoke all extra role members

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests shown enforcing single-member role invariants.

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor RoleGranted events and role member counts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
